questions.q: Who directed the 2014 film Court?
questions.explanation: Court (2014) was written and directed by Chaitanya Tamhane.

questions.q: In which year was the film Court released?
questions.explanation: Court premiered in 2014 at the Venice Film Festival.

questions.q: What is the primary language of the film Court?
questions.explanation: Court is a multilingual film, primarily in Marathi, with Hindi, Gujarati and English dialogue as well.

questions.q: The film Court centres on the trial of a person from which profession?
questions.explanation: Court follows the trial of Narayan Kamble, an ageing folk singer and activist.

questions.q: In the film Court, the elderly folk singer is accused of which alleged offence?
questions.explanation: The folk singer Narayan Kamble is charged with abetting the suicide of a sewer worker through an inflammatory song.

questions.q: In which Indian city is the film Court primarily set?
questions.explanation: Court is set in Mumbai and depicts proceedings in a lower court there.

questions.q: Which prestigious Indian award did the film Court win for Best Feature Film?
questions.explanation: Court won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film at the 62nd National Film Awards.

questions.q: At which major international film festival did the film Court have its world premiere in 2014?
questions.explanation: Court premiered in the Orizzonti section of the 2014 Venice Film Festival.

questions.q: The film Court was selected as India's official entry for the Best Foreign Language Film category at which year's Academy Awards?
questions.explanation: Court was chosen as India's official entry for the 2016 (88th) Academy Awards.

questions.q: What is the genre and tone most associated with the film Court?
questions.explanation: Court is widely described as a restrained, slow-burn courtroom drama with documentary-like realism.

questions.q: What is the name of the ageing folk singer on trial in the film Court?
questions.explanation: The accused folk singer in Court is named Narayan Kamble.

questions.q: Which prize did the film Court win in the Orizzonti (Horizons) section at the Venice Film Festival?
options: The Orizzonti Award for Best Film
questions.explanation: Court won the Orizzonti Award for Best Film at Venice in 2014, along with the Lion of the Future debut award.

questions.q: Court was the feature directorial debut of Chaitanya Tamhane; roughly how old was he when the film premiered in 2014?
questions.explanation: Chaitanya Tamhane, born in 1987, was in his late twenties when Court premiered, making it a notably young debut.

questions.q: Which veteran activist and writer played the folk singer Narayan Kamble in the film Court?
questions.explanation: The role of Narayan Kamble was played by activist and writer Vira Sathidar.

questions.q: Who both produced the film Court and played the defence lawyer Vinay Vora in it?
questions.explanation: Vivek Gomber produced Court and portrayed the human-rights defence lawyer Vinay Vora.

questions.q: In the film Court, the public prosecutor Nutan is portrayed by which actress?
questions.explanation: Geetanjali Kulkarni plays the middle-class public prosecutor Nutan in Court.

questions.q: Court is noted for a visual style built largely on which kind of camera technique?
options: Long, static wide shots held without cutting
questions.explanation: Court is known for its restrained style of long, static wide shots that observe the proceedings without dramatic camera movement.

questions.q: The film Court is widely seen as a critique of which aspect of Indian society?
options: The slow, class-bound machinery of the judicial system
questions.explanation: Court satirises and critiques the sluggish, bureaucratic and class-divided workings of India's lower judicial system.

questions.q: Court used which approach to casting and dialogue to heighten its realism?
options: Entirely improvised dialogue with no script
Dubbing all roles into a single language
Largely non-professional actors and naturalistic, multilingual dialogue
questions.explanation: Court cast many non-professional actors and used naturalistic, multilingual dialogue to achieve a documentary-like authenticity.

questions.q: After its festival run, the film Court received a theatrical release in India in which year?
questions.explanation: Court was released theatrically in India in April 2015, the year after its Venice premiere.

questions.q: Chaitanya Tamhane's follow-up feature after Court, released in 2020, was a film centred on a Hindustani classical musician. What was it titled?
questions.explanation: Chaitanya Tamhane directed 'The Disciple' (2020) as his acclaimed follow-up to Court.

questions.q: The film Court is structured as a portrait of several lives connected to the trial. Whose private life is most prominently followed alongside the defence lawyer's?
options: The public prosecutor and the judge
questions.explanation: Court follows the everyday lives of the defence lawyer, the public prosecutor and the judge outside the courtroom as well as inside it.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer on the film Court?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Desai was the cinematographer of Court.

questions.q: In the film Court, the man whose death the folk singer is accused of abetting worked in which occupation?
questions.explanation: The deceased man in Court was a sewer (manhole) worker, and the prosecution claims the singer's song drove him to suicide.

questions.q: At the 2014 Venice Film Festival, in addition to the Orizzonti Best Film prize, the film Court won which award recognising a debut feature?
options: The Lion of the Future (Luigi De Laurentiis) award
questions.explanation: Court won the Lion of the Future award (Luigi De Laurentiis Award) for a best debut film at Venice 2014.

questions.q: Which acclaimed Mexican filmmaker mentored Chaitanya Tamhane on his post-Court project through the Rolex Mentor and Protege Arts Initiative?
questions.explanation: Alfonso Cuaron mentored Chaitanya Tamhane through the Rolex Mentor and Protege Arts Initiative, working with him on 'The Disciple'.

questions.q: The film Court won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film at which edition of the National Film Awards?
questions.explanation: Court was honoured as Best Feature Film at the 62nd National Film Awards, for films of 2014.

questions.q: Under which production banner, headed by Vivek Gomber, was the film Court produced?
questions.explanation: Court was produced under Vivek Gomber's Zoo Entertainment banner.

questions.q: The film Court was India's Oscar entry for 2016 but, like most Indian entries, did not advance to which stage?
options: The final five Best Foreign Language Film nominees
questions.explanation: Court was submitted as India's entry for the 2016 Academy Awards but was not among the final five nominees in the foreign-language category.

questions.q: Within the broader landscape of Indian cinema, the film Court is most often grouped with which kind of filmmaking?
questions.explanation: Court is regarded as a landmark of contemporary Indian independent and realist (parallel) cinema rather than mainstream commercial filmmaking.
